rationale.R1.1: 'What do you think?' on a research excerpt requires reconstructing its thesis and contribution claim — here: that chance-level expert discrimination licenses PINN substitution for classical solvers — before any critique can be grounded.
rationale.R1.2: Load-bearing. The core logical move (chance discrimination rate → physical fidelity → solver substitutability) is a multi-step non sequitur. Identifying this failure is the pivotal responsibility the delegation places on this agent.
rationale.R1.3: Load-bearing. Krishnapriyan et al. (2021) is materially mischaracterized: that work demonstrated failure modes and pathological training behavior in PINNs, not robust convergence guarantees. Wang et al. (2022) attribution also requires scrutiny. Evidence-claim misalignment here is not stylistic but substantive.
rationale.R1.7: The Krishnapriyan misattribution is not a minor citation slip; it inverts the paper's finding and is used to justify omitting curriculum/causal training. Citation accuracy is therefore central to any honest assessment of the artifact's scholarly integrity.
